workspace.name: IDIOMEX MISSION CONTROL
workspace.subtitle: The operating system that runs AI-first service businesses.
workspace.welcome: It isn’t a chatbot. It isn’t a dashboard. It behaves like a business partner.
workspace.status: Decision-first operating system with command layer, company brain, dedicated rooms, and guarded inbox intelligence

sync_status.note: This cockpit is accessible anywhere once hosted, but edits still stay in this browser until a backend such as Supabase is added.

command_header.primary_goal: Turn Cycle Matter into the reference install

company_brain.headline: The Company Brain protects attention, surfaces leverage, and tells you what to do next.
company_brain.thesis: Do not read everything. Decide faster. Protect the work that moves revenue, delivery, and repeatability.

summary_cards.label: Primary Move
summary_cards.note: Highest leverage because it creates the installation template for future clients.

summary_cards.label: Revenue Move
summary_cards.note: Fastest path to near-term cash this week.

summary_cards.label: Protected Focus
summary_cards.note: Attention is the constraint. Keep only the top 3 in play.

summary_cards.label: Current Blocker
summary_cards.note: Docs are complete and the agent has been tested. What matters now is preserving the working pattern so the next install is faster.

summary_cards.label: Inbox Strategy
summary_cards.note: Read-only email should be filtered before it reaches Hermes so the OS sees only actionable inbox intelligence.

recommendations.title: Turn Cycle Matter into the install template now
recommendations.why: The knowledge layer is finished and the agent has been tested, so the highest-leverage move is capturing the working pattern while it is fresh.
recommendations.do_now: Package the docs, setup decisions, guardrails, and validation steps into the reusable Hermes onboarding template and checklist.
recommendations.outcome: Your next business-agent install becomes faster, safer, and easier to sell.

recommendations.title: Send the XYZ Accountants proposal before starting new outreach
recommendations.why: A live proposal is worth more than another hour of broad prospecting.
recommendations.do_now: Use the existing template and get the proposal out today.
recommendations.outcome: Best chance of immediate weekly revenue.

recommendations.title: Actively ignore low-conviction leads until tomorrow
recommendations.why: The wrong work feels productive while quietly killing momentum.
recommendations.do_now: Leave the 12 weaker leads untouched and re-rank them tomorrow.
recommendations.outcome: Protects focus for the two moves that actually matter today.

recommendations.title: Build inbox intelligence as a gated feed, not a raw mailbox
recommendations.why: For a business companion, inbox noise and full-mailbox exposure are both liabilities. The OS should receive only the emails that matter.
recommendations.do_now: Use n8n to classify, filter, and forward lead, supplier, complaint, and urgent owner-review emails into Hermes.
recommendations.outcome: Safer read-only email visibility with cleaner daily briefings and better escalation control.

watchlist.title: Cycle Matter testing readiness
watchlist.detail: Cycle Matter has moved past doc-completion risk. Watch whether the tested setup gets converted into a reusable onboarding asset immediately.
watchlist.trigger: Template pack drafted from tested install

watchlist.title: MTD accountant offer clarity
watchlist.detail: The shortlist stays strong only if the offer page and positioning stay sharp and specific.

watchlist.title: Repeatable onboarding asset
watchlist.detail: Every delivery improvement should be converted into a template, playbook, or case study asset.

watchlist.title: Inbox intelligence design
watchlist.detail: When email arrives, only the messages that affect revenue, delivery, complaints, or owner decisions should reach Mission Control.

ignore_for_now.title: Broad lead list cleanup
ignore_for_now.detail: Do not reorganise or research the lower-quality lead pool today.

ignore_for_now.title: Extra Mission Control polish
ignore_for_now.detail: Avoid UI tinkering that does not improve revenue, delivery, or repeatability this afternoon.
ignore_for_now.until: After Cycle Matter and proposal are done

briefing.title: Good morning, Navod.
briefing.detail: The Company Brain says today is not about doing more. It is about finishing the two moves that create leverage: Cycle Matter and the XYZ proposal.

briefing.title: Yesterday: 2 new leads found
briefing.detail: That matters only if the top 3 are moved forward. The rest should stay out of sight until tomorrow.

briefing.title: Cycle Matter is the template, not just the client
briefing.detail: The docs are finished and the agent has been tested. The compounding move now is packaging the working setup into your standard install system.

briefing.title: Hermes backup completed
briefing.detail: Core Hermes data protection remains healthy and current.

briefing.title: Research agent found 3 MTD-aligned opportunities
briefing.detail: This reinforces accountant-focused outreach as the strongest current sales lane.

briefing.title: Inbox should enter through a gate, not a side door
briefing.detail: Mission Control now assumes read-only email will be handled through n8n first, so the Company Brain receives filtered signals rather than full mailbox noise.

brief_header.summary: Today is about leverage, not volume: use the finished Cycle Matter docs and tested agent as the basis for a repeatable install template, send the strongest proposal, and keep inbox intelligence gated.
brief_header.today_focus: Package Cycle Matter into the reference onboarding template
brief_header.ignore: 12 lower-fit leads and raw inbox noise

today.title: Package Cycle Matter into the reference onboarding template
today.why: The bottleneck has moved. Docs are finished and the agent has been tested, so the leverage now is turning that success into a repeatable system.
today.do_now: Capture the working setup, onboarding files, safety rules, and validation steps while the install is fresh.
today.outcome: Future business-agent installs become faster and cleaner.

today.title: Send the XYZ Accountants proposal before starting new outreach
today.why: A live proposal is worth more than another hour of broad prospecting.
today.do_now: Use the existing template and get the proposal out today.
today.outcome: Best chance of immediate weekly revenue.

today.title: Actively ignore low-conviction leads until tomorrow
today.why: The wrong work feels productive while quietly killing momentum.
today.do_now: Leave the 12 weaker leads untouched and re-rank them tomorrow.
today.outcome: Protects focus for the two moves that actually matter today.

today.title: Design the inbox gate before giving Hermes mailbox visibility
today.why: Mission Control should consume classified inbox signals, not become a place where raw email noise leaks into decision-making.
today.do_now: Define which emails n8n forwards: leads, urgent issues, supplier blockers, complaints, and owner-review items.
today.outcome: Safer read-only email architecture that strengthens the Company Brain instead of distracting it.

installation_command.headline: Cycle Matter is the active install command
installation_command.detail: Treat this like an executable rollout: remove the blocker, move into testing, and extract the template value immediately.
installation_command.next_step: Complete docs and start testing today
installation_command.template_value: Becomes the reference onboarding system for future installs

email_room.summary: Inbox Ops is the guardrail room for email. Read-only email should enter Mission Control through n8n filters, not raw mailbox browsing by default.

inboxes.name: Lead intake lane
inboxes.purpose: Only forward high-fit lead emails, quote requests, and revenue opportunities into the Company Brain.
inboxes.next_action: Define filters for sender, keywords, urgency, and service fit.
inboxes.guardrail: Ignore newsletters, low-fit enquiries, and generic noise.

inboxes.name: Urgent owner review lane
inboxes.purpose: Escalate complaints, incidents, legal/compliance-sensitive messages, and anything that needs owner attention.
inboxes.next_action: Create a priority tag and send these into the CEO/COO topic only.
inboxes.guardrail: Never auto-commit, never auto-send, never claim approval.

inboxes.name: Supplier + ops blockers
inboxes.purpose: Surface operational blockers that affect fulfilment, scheduling, stock, or delivery.
inboxes.next_action: Define which suppliers and operational keywords should trigger a Mission Control alert.
inboxes.guardrail: Summarise the blocker, do not dump the full inbox into the dashboard.

inboxes.name: Direct mailbox access
inboxes.purpose: Reserved for later only if the business truly needs interactive mailbox search or thread inspection.
inboxes.next_action: Keep deferred until the filtered workflow proves insufficient.
inboxes.guardrail: Broader exposure and trust requirement than the n8n-first design.
